mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the Gauss Elimination Method?

options.text: To solve systems of nonlinear equations
To solve systems of linear equations
To find the inverse of a matrix
To solve linear programming problems

mcqs.explanation: The Gauss Elimination Method is used to solve systems of linear equations.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of the Gauss Elimination Method?
mcqs.explanation: The time complexity of the Gauss Elimination Method is O(n^3), where n is the number of variables.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of the Gauss Elimination Method?

options.text: O(n)

mcqs.explanation: The space complexity of the Gauss Elimination Method is O(n^2), where n is the number of variables.

mcqs.question: What is the limitation of the Gauss Elimination Method?

options.text: It is not suitable for small systems of equations
It is not suitable for large systems of equations
It is not suitable for systems of nonlinear equations
It is not suitable for systems of linear equations

mcqs.explanation: The Gauss Elimination Method is not suitable for large systems of equations due to its high time and space complexity.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between the Gauss Elimination Method and the Gauss-Jordan Method?

options.text: The Gauss-Jordan Method is a variation of the Gauss Elimination Method that transforms the augmented matrix into reduced row echelon form
The Gauss-Jordan Method is a variation of the Gauss Elimination Method that transforms the augmented matrix into upper triangular form
The Gauss-Jordan Method is a variation of the Gauss Elimination Method that transforms the augmented matrix into lower triangular form
The Gauss-Jordan Method is a variation of the Gauss Elimination Method that transforms the augmented matrix into diagonal form

mcqs.explanation: The Gauss-Jordan Method is a variation of the Gauss Elimination Method that transforms the augmented matrix into reduced row echelon form.
